task:
ATOM 1 of the founder-approved CODE-fix for Doc Truth Analysis. CONTEXT (binding founder decision 2026-06-27, Option B): the live `commitOnlyScope: true` atom hold-back is a CONFIRMED REGRESSION from ADR-0023. ADR-0023 Amendment 2 stands: the commit spine commits EVERYTHING the actor changed; out-of-lane paths are committed and FLAGGED, never withheld. Write-scope is advisory (flag), never enforced by withholding files. Proof: the README was dropped three times by the hold-back guard.

WHAT TO CHANGE: In `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts`, restore commit-everything-and-flag for verified-atom commits. The hold-back is driven by `commitOnlyScope: true` (founder noted approx lines 260 and 488, but DO NOT trust line numbers — they drift). FIRST run `grep -rn 'commitOnlyScope' packages/` and enumerate the COMPLETE site set yourself, including any synced/twin copies, the type/interface that declares the field, and the consuming code in the commit spine that branches on it. Then neutralize the hold-back so the atom lane commits the whole changed set and FLAGS out-of-lane paths (the existing `commitScoped`/commit-everything-and-flag behavior), instead of withholding out-of-scope files.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. After the change, a verified atom commits every path the actor changed; out-of-lane paths are committed AND surfaced/flagged in the receipt, never withheld/dropped.
2. `grep -rn 'commitOnlyScope' packages/` shows no remaining site that still WITHHOLDS files (the flag is removed or neutralized to advisory-flag-only; if the field is fully dead, remove it and its type declaration).
3. Write-scope remains advisory: out-of-lane paths are flagged, not used to suppress the commit.
4. `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` was asserting the CORRECT behavior all along — it must now exit 0 with NO edit to the script. Run it and paste the exit code.
5. Run the runner / commit-spine test suites and typecheck; paste the actual commands, exit codes, and a real artifact (file/diff) as evidence.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not weaken or delete any commit-spine test to make it pass; do not change `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs`; do not touch the docs in this atom (docs revert is a separate atom); preserve unrelated runner behavior. WRITE-SCOPE: `packages/core/src/runner/**` (and the minimal type/interface site that declares `commitOnlyScope` if it lives elsewhere under `packages/core/**`). Report the full diff and the command evidence.

ATOM 2 of the Doc Truth Analysis CODE-fix run: revert the doc edits that described the now-removed commit-gate hold-back as current truth. CONTEXT: atom 1 (commit d413569) restored always-commit-and-flag in the commit spine — verified-atom commits land the WHOLE changed set and FLAG out-of-lane paths; write-scope is advisory and NEVER suppresses a commit (ADR-0023 Amendment 2; founder decision 2026-06-27, Option B). Earlier in this priority (run_271 Atom A) several docs were bent to describe the buggy 'held back / left in the working tree / withheld / not committed' behavior as current truth. Those passages are now WRONG and must be reverted to the always-commit-and-flag truth.

FILES TO FIX (named by the founder spec):
1. ARCHITECTURE.md — the commit-spine section.
2. docs/glossary.md — the write-scope entry.
3. docs/fault-injection-live-proofs.md
4. docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md
5. docs/oz-improvement-routing.md

DEFECT-CLASS SWEEP (do not trust only the list above): run `grep -rniE 'held[ -]?back|withheld|left in the working tree|not committed|hold(s)? out-of' ARCHITECTURE.md docs/` and inspect every hit. Anywhere a doc says write-scope/out-of-lane paths are held back / withheld / left dirty / not committed as CURRENT behavior, correct it to: 'out-of-lane (out-of-scope) paths are committed AND flagged for visibility; write-scope is advisory and never withholds files.' Leave alone any hit that is correctly describing HISTORY (an explicitly past-tense/superseded note), the audit-write-boundary hard stop (Takeover audits — a genuinely different mechanism that DOES refuse), or the in-scope pre-run dirty-guard (which refuses a launch, not a commit). When in doubt about whether a hit is the audit boundary vs the advisory write-scope, preserve the audit-boundary text.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. After your edits, no governed doc under ARCHITECTURE.md or docs/ states (as current truth) that out-of-lane/out-of-scope agent edits are held back, withheld, or left uncommitted. They are committed and flagged.
2. The corrected text matches the restored code (commit-gate/gate.ts + workspace-commit.ts: committable = the whole changed set) and ADR-0023 Amendment 2 / ADR-0007 (write-scope advisory).
3. The audit-write-boundary and pre-run dirty-guard descriptions are preserved (do not rewrite those into 'commits everything').

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: docs only — do NOT touch any code under packages/** or scripts/**. If any doc has a behavior-pinned test/fixture (e.g. a contract or routing-guide assertion), run the affected suite and paste exit code; otherwise note none apply. WRITE-SCOPE: docs/** and ARCHITECTURE.md. Report the full diff and the grep output (before and after) as evidence.
